steps.title: This protocol performs the Simplified Fe Quantification Assay
steps.instruction: This protocol performs the Simplified Fe Quantification Assay. If in Test Mode, the protocol will skip over all incubations and temperature changes. The protocol will automatically pause if tips have to be replaced, prompting the user. Pauses are included according to the protocol.

steps.title: User calibrates protocol on deck
steps.instruction: User calibrates protocol on deck.

steps.title: Deck starts with samples in the...
steps.instruction: Deck starts with samples in the sample block and empty digestion plate in the heating module. The reagent block is on the deck but only has water in Well A1.

steps.title: Pipette 5-20 uL of water in...
steps.instruction: Pipette 5-20 uL of water in triplicate from the reagent block into the digestion plate. (1 x 30 uL tip). Make the pipetted volume user selectable and consistent for all samples.

steps.title: Pipette 5-20 uL (same as in...
steps.instruction: Pipette 5-20 uL (same as in step 3) of each sample in triplicate from the sample block into the digestion plate. (1-24 x 30 uL tips)

steps.title: Add 1200 uL of nitric acid...
steps.instruction: Add 1200 uL of nitric acid from the acid block into each well of the digestion plate. The solution is mixed 15 times at 200ul with the multi-channel pipette

steps.title: Pause so user can cover the digestion plate
steps.instruction: Pause so user can cover the digestion plate.

steps.title: Raise heating block temperature to 95...
steps.instruction: Raise heating block temperature to 95 °C (this is actually now variable, per email on 12/15/22) and maintain for 12 h (this is actually now variable, per email on 12/15/22).

steps.title: Cool heating block to room temperature
steps.instruction: Cool heating block to room temperature. Wait for 1 h to ensure all samples reach room temperature.

steps.title: Pause until user removes the cover...
steps.instruction: Pause until user removes the cover from the digestion plate and places the reagent block and analysis plate on the deck.

steps.title: Transfer triplicate 10 uL aliquots from...
steps.instruction: Transfer triplicate 10 uL aliquots from the 7 Fe calibration standards in the sample block into the Quartz analysis plate in slot 2. (21 wells; 7 x 30 uL tips)

steps.title: All columns in digestion plate mixed with multi-channel pipette
steps.instruction: All columns in digestion plate mixed with multi-channel pipette.

steps.title: Transfer 5-20 uL aliquots from each...
steps.instruction: Transfer 5-20 uL aliquots from each sample in the digestion plate into the Quartz analysis plate in slot 2. (6-75 wells; 6-75 x 30 uL tips).

steps.title: Pause for user to place the...
steps.instruction: Pause for user to place the Quartz analysis plate in the heating module.

steps.title: Raise heating block temperature to 95...
steps.instruction: Raise heating block temperature to 95 °C and maintain for 1 h.

steps.title: Cool heating block to room temperature
steps.instruction: Cool heating block to room temperature. Wait for 1 h to ensure all samples reach room temperature.

steps.title: Pause for user to place the...
steps.instruction: Pause for user to place the Quartz analysis plate in slot 2 and place the digestion plate in the heating module.

steps.title: Add 46 uL of water from...
steps.instruction: Add 46 uL of water from the reagent block to each well in the Quartz analysis plate in slot 2. (27-96 wells; 1 x 300 uL tip)

steps.title: Add 30 uL reagent A from...
steps.instruction: Add 30 uL reagent A from the reagent block to each well in the Quartz analysis plate in slot 2. (27-96 wells) a. Mixing step.

steps.title: Wait 1 h for reagent A...
steps.instruction: Wait 1 h for reagent A to react with Fe in each well.

steps.title: Add 49 uL of reagent B...
steps.instruction: Add 49 uL of reagent B from the reagent block to each well in the Quartz analysis plate in slot 2. (27-96 wells; 1 x 300 uL tip)

steps.title: Add 75 uL of reagent C...
steps.instruction: Add 75 uL of reagent C from the reagent block to each well in the Quartz analysis plate in slot 2. (27-96 wells) a. Mixing step.
